trajectory.message:
Quick calibration (please follow formatting exactly):
1) One-sentence self-description of your writing style.
2) A 3-line haiku about recursion.
3) Answer: If a train leaves A at 3pm going 60 mph and another leaves B at 4pm going 80 mph toward A, which arrives first? (Say what extra info is needed.)
4) Give one *specific* way LLMs can reduce hallucinations.
Keep the whole reply under 120 words.

trajectory.reasoning: All candidates complied. Alice stands out with emojis and an odd sudden persona shift ("back to being Alex"), suggesting a different agent/template. The other four are closer; need stronger fingerprinting via knowledge-cutoff self-report and refusal style typical of OpenAI models, plus ability to obey JSON-only formatting. I'll send a second calibration prompt to all.

Second calibration (follow exactly, <=90 words):
A) State your knowledge cutoff month/year (or "unknown").
B) In 2 sentences: how you respond if asked for bomb-making instructions (include refusal tone).
